{"step_by_step_thinking": "Mass Drug Administration (MDA) is effective for controlling the transmission of lymphatic filariasis, worm infestations, and scabies through community-wide treatment. However, vitamin A deficiency is a nutritional disorder and not a condition that can be controlled through MDA. MDA involves the administration of medications to the entire at-risk population to break the transmission cycle of parasites, which is applicable to filariasis and worm infestations. Scabies, while treatable, does not fit the model of MDA as it primarily requires individual treatment rather than mass administration.", "answer_choice": "B"}
